video_description: ✨QOTD: Do you have any bad bookish habits?✨ No books were harmed THAT much (at least not more than they already were) in the making of this video👀 Ok first off,  I apologise for committing such crimes against Lizzie Blake…if it helps, she’s a 5 star from me and I love her dearly ( 10/10 reccommend!) i’m just a terrible booklover🤣 Secondly, i’ve had the plan of doing this video in my head since the first time I heard anti hero and I just haven’t gotten round to it yet partly because this is the first time doing a video with my face in front of the camera rather than behind🤣 so yes… it’s me… hi👋🏻👋🏻 Thirdly, please don’t hate me too much and banish me from booktok, bookstagram or any other bookish community… I would be very sad🤣 #booktok #booktokker #bookhabits #bookishpetpeeves #bookishthings #readersofbooktok #readersoftiktok #booklover #bookloversoftiktok #dogearing #badbookhabits #antihero #bookishcontent #bookishfyp #bookfyp #bookishcommunity #bookishpost #bookrecs #bookrecommendations #bookrecommendation #5starreads #bookobsessed #readersgonnaread #bookstagrammer #bookstagram #booktokuk #booktokkeruk #bookishvibes #bookishlife #romancebooks #romancebooktok

voice_to_text: let's talk about my physical book icks or things I can tell you with pretty solid certainty that I plan to never do to my own physical books now I'm gonna say this now that we don't have to repeat myself later but if you do any of these things that is totally fine there's no judgment these are just the things that make me feel physically ill thinking about doing to my own books so to start it off strong breaking the spines are you really telling me there are people here who just and like break a book open no literally like I said makes me wanna throw up I have explained before that I can barely like I need to read like this the idea of breaking a spine just hurts my body and soul and the only thing I would ever consider doing that I have seen some people do to sort of break the spine or open the book up a little bit is doing that thing where you take each cover down and then slowly bring pages down and press things down and that way you've sort of got a floppier more open book but even that scares me a little I do not want creases here cracks it's just a personal preference I can't do it so next in this is probably my least favorite one and maybe the only one I would judge you for because look if you are dogearing your pages like I said I it makes me want to barf like use anything it does not matter if you don't have money and you don't have fancy bookmarks like you know I have a couple here which is fun and all you can use a piece of toilet paper you can use a tissue you can use a banana a phone it does not matter but if you're still out here folding your pages over and like bending the pages in your book please please seek help now when it comes to annotating books I have made a video about how I'm tabbed my first book but literally all that involved was putting tabs inside the pages where I found quotes that I liked and wanted to remember even then I don't write on the tabs but anytime I see someone really highlighting or writing in the books it just hurts everything in me like I maybe it's from like when I was younger and people writing in textbooks I just hated when they would do that and then I would try to read through their writing over the history textbook you know what I mean there's always been something in me that cannot stand writing in books and the same you know is the case now I just can't understand people who are doing something so permanent you are highlighting and writing in pen in that book and that cannot be undone I I just don't think I will ever do it or get used to it I think I can only tab that's like the most I can do alright so this last one here is less about like a singular book and what you might be doing to that book and more about organization of your shelf but if you organize your books by color I just I I don't really get it now look I am Type A I'm very organized I get the aesthetic view of it I love color coding things I'm literally the kid who used to put her M&M’s in color coded order before I would eat them so I sort of get that but you're telling me that you have like a four or five book series let's just use ACOTAR for example and all the books are different colors and you're telling me that you have them all separated at different parts of your shelf so anytime you wanna read the series you're having to pull them out from different parts I it like hurts my brain so yeah take all that with what you will but I will not be doing any of that anytime soon to any of my books
